# Task 11 serialization implementation (2026-03-13 05:47Z)

_**User**_

Implement Task 11 from docs/plans/2026-03-13-dev-shell-impl-plan.md.

**Task 11: .bin 序列化 — 格式与序列化（TDD）**

1. Create tests/serialization_tests.rs with:
   use dev_shell::vfs::Vfs;
   use dev_shell::serialization;
   #[test]
   fn roundtrip_empty_vfs() {
       let vfs = Vfs::new();
       let bytes = serialization::serialize(&vfs).unwrap();
       assert!(bytes.starts_with(b"DEVS"));
       let loaded = serialization::deserialize(&bytes).unwrap();
       assert!(loaded.resolve_absolute("/").is_ok());
   }

2. In src/lib.rs add: pub mod serialization;

3. Create src/serialization.rs with:
   - Format: magic "DEVS" (4 bytes) + version u8 (1) + cwd length u32 + cwd UTF-8 bytes + root node (recursive).
   - Node: 1 byte type (0 = Dir, 1 = File) + name length u16 (little-endian) + name UTF-8 + if Dir: u32 child count + recurse for each child; if File: u64 content length + raw bytes.
   - serialize(vfs: &Vfs) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> and deserialize(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vfs, Error>. Use a simple enum error or std::io::Error.
   - Implement so roundtrip_empty_vfs passes. Add a second test: roundtrip with mkdir and write_file (e.g. /foo/bar dir and /foo/f file with content "hi") then serialize, deserialize, and assert tree matches.

4. Run: cargo test roundtrip and cargo test serialization

5. Commit: git add src/serialization.rs src/lib.rs tests/serialization_tests.rs && git commit -m "feat(serialization): .bin format serialize/deserialize"

Work directory: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/dev_shell
Report: format used, any issues, test results, commit.
